sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Fermi Inc. Investors with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead Class Action Lawsuit, Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP Announces
feed.summary: Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP has announced a class action lawsuit against Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ: FRMI) for investors who suffered substantial losses. The lawsuit alleges that Fermi made false or misleading statements in its IPO documents concerning tenant demand for its Project Matador campus and the reliance on a single tenant's funding, which was later terminated. Investors who purchased Fermi stock during the Class Period (October 1, 2025 – December 11, 2025) have until March 6, 2026, to seek appointment as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust Sees Weekly Outflows Amid Tech Volatility
feed.summary: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) experienced a 0.35% drop and $9.26 billion in net outflows over the past week, partly due to volatility in its major tech holdings. Nvidia, despite short-term fluctuations, maintains a Strong Buy rating due to strong AI demand, while Apple, heading into Q1 2026 earnings, shows robust iPhone demand and services growth, earning it a Moderate Buy. Microsoft is poised for strong Q2 2026 results, driven by AI and Azure, leading to a Strong Buy rating despite recent valuation concerns.

feed.title: AutoZone Stock: Can This Relentless Buyback Machine Keep Beating The Market?
feed.summary: AutoZone has continued its market outperformance, driven by aggressive share buybacks and consistent demand for auto parts due to an aging vehicle fleet. Despite the stock trading near record highs, analysts remain largely bullish, citing the company's strong cash flow, effective capital allocation strategy, and resilient business model. The company's focus on the commercial market and improving operational efficiencies are expected to sustain its growth, making it a compelling long-term investment.

feed.title: Dem-Backed ICE Body Cams Are a Giveaway to Weapons Makers
feed.summary: Democratic legislators are proposing body cameras for ICE agents to reduce misconduct, but the article argues this is a windfall for weapons technology companies like Axon Enterprise, Inc., with little evidence of efficacy. Axon has lobbied extensively for the $20 million allocated for body cameras in the Department of Homeland Security spending bill, despite facing lawsuits for monopolizing the market and raising prices. Critics suggest the bill primarily benefits contractors and does little to address accountability or curb ICE's actions.

feed.title: Oxbow Advisors LLC Lowers Holdings in Frontline PLC $FRO
feed.summary: Oxbow Advisors LLC significantly reduced its stake in Frontline PLC by 46.4% in the third quarter, selling 181,293 shares. Despite this, other institutions like Marshall Wace and SG Americas increased their holdings, with hedge funds and institutional investors now owning about 22.7% of the company. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $24.62, while the stock currently trades near $26.10 and offers an annualized dividend yield of 2.9%.

feed.title: CRDF - Trovagene Presents Outlook for Second Half of 2018
feed.summary: Trovagene (NASDAQ: TROV), an oncology therapeutics company, provided an outlook for the second half of 2018, emphasizing progress in two key clinical trials for PCM-075 targeting Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML) and metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer (mCRPC). The company highlighted significant achievements in the first half of 2018, including encouraging preliminary clinical data and progress in trial enrollments. Trovagene aims to complete dose escalation, present safety/efficacy data, and initiate Phase 2 segments of its AML trial, while also evaluating strategic partnerships and searching for a new CEO.

feed.title: Costco stock (COST) ends Friday higher; what to watch before the next U.S. session
feed.summary: Costco (COST) shares closed Friday 0.7% higher at $983.25, just under the $1,000 mark. Investors are looking forward to several key dates, including a dividend record date on January 30, the release of January sales figures on February 4, and the fiscal second-quarter earnings call on March 5. Additionally, the company faces a new class-action lawsuit regarding "no preservatives" claims on its rotisserie chicken products.

feed.title: Gencor Sets 2026 Annual Meeting and Governance Deadlines
feed.summary: Gencor Industries has announced the scheduling of its Annual Meeting of Stockholders for April 3, 2026, for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2025. Due to the later date, the company has updated its key governance timelines, setting February 26, 2026, as the record date and February 2, 2026, as the deadline for shareholder proposals and director nominations. A recent analyst rating on GENC stock is a Buy with a $15.50 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates GENC as Neutral, noting strong financial quality but weak cash flow consistency.

feed.title: UPDATE on the Walt Disney Company’s Search for New CEO
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company is actively searching for a successor to CEO Bob Iger, with an announcement expected in early 2026, possibly at the March 16th shareholders' meeting. Iger's salary increased by 12% to $45.8 million in 2025, comprising base salary, stock awards, option awards, and bonuses. Top contenders for the CEO position include Josh D’Amaro, Chairman of Disney Experiences, and Dana Walden, Co-Chair of Disney Entertainment.

feed.title: Axon Enterprise Inc. Is Quietly Taking Over – But Is This Stock a Hidden Cheat Code or Overhyped C
feed.summary: Axon Enterprise Inc., known for its tasers and body cameras, is gaining significant attention and trading near all-time highs. The company benefits from a unique position at the intersection of tech, policing, and viral content, leveraging its hardware with a strong recurring software revenue model through its Axon Cloud platform. While its valuation is high, investors are drawn to its robust growth, profitability, and essential role in modern public safety.

feed.summary: Cencora Inc is gaining quiet attention from Wall Street despite not being a mainstream name, due to its critical role as a global pharmaceutical distributor. The article highlights its significant scale, focus on specialty pharmaceuticals and services, and stable revenue model within the healthcare sector. Investors are eyeing Cencora as a long-term, fundamentals-first play with defensive potential, rather than a high-hype stock.

feed.title: Tesla's FSD Price Hike, GM's Production Shift, Boeing's Contract Surge And More: This Week In Mobility
feed.summary: This article summarizes the major mobility news of the week, including Tesla's FSD price increase as the technology improves, General Motors' decision to end production of the Chevrolet Bolt and shift other vehicle manufacturing, and Boeing's significant surge in government procurement contracts. It also covers an NHTSA probe into GM vehicles for engine failures and Tesla's Q4 earnings estimates.

feed.title: ROSEN, A LEADING INVESTOR RIGHTS LAW FIRM, Encourages Varonis Systems, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - VRNS
feed.summary: Rosen Law Firm is reminding investors of Varonis Systems, Inc. who purchased securities between February 4, 2025, and October 28, 2025, about the upcoming March 9, 2026, lead plaintiff deadline for a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Varonis made materially false and/or misleading statements regarding its ability to maintain ARR projections while converting customers to SaaS, and its capacity to retain customers on its platform. The firm encourages affected investors to secure counsel and consider joining the class action to potentially recover damages.

feed.title: ROSEN, A RANKED AND LEADING LAW FIRM, Encourages Ardent Health, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - ARDT
feed.summary: The Rosen Law Firm is reminding investors of Ardent Health, Inc. (NYSE: ARDT) who purchased securities between July 18, 2024, and November 12, 2025, about the March 9, 2026 lead plaintiff deadline in a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Ardent Health made misrepresentations regarding its accounts receivable and professional malpractice liability insurance, leading to investor damages. Investors are encouraged to contact the firm to secure counsel and potentially recover losses.

feed.title: Woman sues HomeGoods in Ocala after tripping over employee working on rug
feed.summary: A Marion County woman has filed a lawsuit against The TJX Companies, Inc., parent company of HomeGoods, after allegedly tripping over an employee working on a rug at a store in Ocala. Kathryn Monaco is seeking over $50,000 in damages for bodily injury and suffering, claiming the company was negligent and created a dangerous condition. The incident occurred on October 3, 2025, at the HomeGoods located at Market Street at Heathbrook.

feed.title: The Truth About Generac Holdings: Backup Power Stock That Everyone Slept On
feed.summary: Generac Holdings is a company leveraging the increasing demand for home backup power due to climate change and grid instability. While not a trendy meme stock, it's a volatile, mid-to-large-cap company with real products and services, positioning itself as a core play in the energy resilience sector. The article examines Generac's market position, competitive landscape, and investment potential for those seeking exposure to solutions for unreliable power grids.

feed.title: C&F Bank taps Navy veteran and VCU professor for board role
feed.summary: C&F Financial Corporation (NASDAQ: CFFI) has announced the appointment of Dr. David H. Downs to its Board of Directors and the board of C&F Bank, effective February 24, 2026. Dr. Downs, an Alfred L. Blake Endowed Chair Professor at Virginia Commonwealth University and former U.S. Navy officer and management consultant, brings expertise in real estate, investments, and risk management. He has served on the C&F Central Virginia Advisory Board for over 12 years.

feed.title: Why Anywhere Real Estate (HOUS) Is Up 24.6% After Voluntary Deregistration And Index Removal And What's Next
feed.summary: Anywhere Real Estate (HOUS) recently saw a 24.6% stock increase following its voluntary deregistration with the SEC and removal from Russell and S&P indices. This move signals its exit from public reporting, shifting its investment narrative from independent stock performance to its integration within the Compass group. Investors are now focused on Compass's capital allocation and integration decisions, as well as Anywhere Real Estate's underlying financial health amidst differing fair value estimates.

feed.title: Hancock Whitney outlines 5%–6% net interest income growth and mid-single digit loan expansion for 2026 amid bond portfolio restructuring
feed.summary: Hancock Whitney (NASDAQ: HWC) has projected a net interest income (NII) growth of 5%–6% and mid-single-digit loan expansion for 2026. This outlook comes after the company announced a balance sheet restructuring that involved selling approximately $600 million in available-for-sale (AFS) securities, incurring various charges and impacting capital ratios, though it aims to remove interest rate sensitivity and reinvest the proceeds.

feed.title: Hecate Energy To Go Public Via EGH Acquisition Deal Valuing Developer At $1.2 Billion
feed.summary: Hecate Energy Group, a U.S. utility-scale energy infrastructure developer, plans to go public by merging with EGH Acquisition Corp. The deal values Hecate at $1.2 billion and will list the combined company on Nasdaq under the ticker “HCTE.” This move aims to accelerate project development, access public capital markets, and address the growing demand for power in industries like data centers.

feed.title: Guggenheim reduces PT on Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) to $1,161, reiterates 'Buy' rating
feed.summary: Guggenheim has reiterated its 'Buy' rating for Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) but has slightly reduced its price target to $1,161. This adjustment indicates continued confidence in the stock's performance despite a minor revision to the target price. Investors will likely view this as a positive signal for Eli Lilly's prospects.

feed.title: Red Cross gets $100K boost to open Carolinas warming shelters
feed.summary: The Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a $100,000 rapid response grant to the American Red Cross to aid communities in North Carolina and South Carolina ahead of Winter Storm Fern. These funds will support warming shelters, provide emergency supplies, and assist community-run shelter operations as severe cold weather is anticipated. This initiative is part of Duke Energy's broader commitment to customer support and storm readiness, reinforcing community resilience.
